para_12: You want to look successful. So you buy things to prove it. Nice car. Designer clothes. Big house. You post photos to show your life is good. Social media makes this worse. You see what everyone else has and feel pressure to keep up. In the US, status is measured by what you own. But chasing status keeps you broke. You spend on things that don't bring real happiness or security. They bring temporary validation that doesn't last. Soon someone has something newer, and you're back to feeling behind. The more you chase, the more you need to spend. You're working to pay for things that make you look successful while actually keeping you financially trapped. Breaking this habit means getting honest. Are you buying this because you want it or because you want people to think well of you? Most of the time, it's the second. Learning to stop caring what others think is hard, but it's the only way to escape the status trap. Real success is financial security, not the appearance of it.
